notes: All 6 steps + prerequisite completed. 230 tests pass (227 orchestrator + 1 e2e + 2 lazy-import). Key discovery: packages with empty __init__.py (analyze, plan, source) required pre-imports in test files for @patch to find submodule attributes.
All 3 steps completed. Parametrized test_update_gate_mode_persists[pause/notify] replaces two individual tests. Fixed .get() to direct dict access for consistency. 37 tests pass.

Completed final 2 steps: template relabel input test (step-11) and cluster_card.html implementation (step-12). Added inline editable input/textarea for label/description in non-excluded cluster cards, wired relabel button with hx-vals js: pattern for JSON submission. All 38 tests pass.

summary: Write failing test for _parse_cluster malformed JSON handling: import _parse_cluster from review module, call with {'clip_ids_json': '{bad json'}, verify it returns empty clip_ids list and clip_count=0 instead of raising json.JSONDecodeError. Also test with clip_ids_json=None still returns empty list (preserves existing behavior).; In _parse_cluster (review.py:222), wrap the json.loads(str(raw)) call in try/except json.JSONDecodeError, defaulting to empty list on failure — matching the existing 'if raw else []' fallback pattern.; Write failing tests for classify gate approve condition on /review/clusters page: (a) setup classify gate='waiting' with 2 clusters, 0 excluded — page SHOULD contain 'Approve Gate' button; (b) setup classify gate='waiting' with 2 clusters, all 2 excluded — page should NOT contain 'Approve Gate'. Use _setup_classify_gate_app helper. Currently test (a) fails because the inverted condition requires len(non_excluded)==0.; In clusters_page (review.py:243), change show_approve_gate condition from 'len(non_excluded) == 0' to 'len(non_excluded) > 0'. Approve gate shows when reviewed clusters exist to proceed with, not when all are excluded.; Write failing test for merge clip_id deduplication: seed c-1 with clip_ids_json='["a","b"]' and c-2 with clip_ids_json='["b","c","d"]' (note 'b' is shared). Merge them and assert clip_ids has no duplicates — exactly 4 unique clips, not 5. Assert len(data['clip_ids']) == len(set(data['clip_ids'])).; In api_merge_clusters (review.py:378-380), replace the simple extend() loop with deduplication using dict.fromkeys(): 'all_clip_ids = list(dict.fromkeys(cid for pc in parsed_clusters for cid in pc["clip_ids"]))'.; Write failing test for timestamp comparison in merge: seed c-1 with time_start='2025-06-15T22:00:00+10:00' and time_end='2025-06-15T23:00:00+10:00', seed c-2 with time_start='2025-06-15T14:00:00+00:00' and time_end='2025-06-16T02:00:00+00:00'. Chronologically c-1 starts earlier (12:00 UTC) but sorts later lexicographically. Assert merged time_start matches c-1's value. Test currently fails because lexicographic min picks c-2.; In api_merge_clusters (review.py:383-386), replace lexicographic min/max with datetime-aware comparison. Add 'from datetime import datetime' import. Use 'min(time_starts, key=datetime.fromisoformat)' and 'max(time_ends, key=datetime.fromisoformat)'. This preserves original string format while comparing chronologically.; Write test for merge transaction safety: seed 2 clusters, use monkeypatch to make db.delete_activity_cluster raise RuntimeError after update has been applied. Call merge endpoint — expect error response. Then read both clusters directly from DB and verify they still exist with original data unchanged (the update was rolled back along with the failed delete).; In api_merge_clusters (review.py:388-404), wrap the mutation block (update_activity_cluster + delete loop) in 'with db:' context manager. Remove the explicit db.conn.commit() call — CatalogDB.__exit__ handles commit-on-success and rollback-on-exception. Keep the post-merge read (get_activity_cluster) inside the with block before __exit__ commits.; Write failing test for cluster_card template relabel inputs: render the cluster card partial for a non-excluded cluster (via HTMX relabel or exclude returning HTML, or direct template rendering) and verify HTML contains: (a) an input element with name='label' and the cluster's current label as value, (b) a textarea/input with name='description' and the cluster's current description as value. These must be associated with the relabel button so values are submitted.; Update cluster_card.html: for non-excluded clusters, replace static h3 label display with <input type='text' name='label' value='...'> and replace static p description with <textarea name='description'>...</textarea>, styled as inline-editable text. Wire the relabel button using hx-vals='js:{...}' to read values from sibling inputs and hx-headers='{"Content-Type":"application/json"}' pattern, or an onclick handler that POSTs JSON and swaps outerHTML — following the JS hx-vals approach used by the merge button in clusters.html.

Added _parse_ts helper for malformed timestamp handling. Step 13: wrote two failing tests (malformed time_start and time_end) confirming 500 instead of 422. Step 14: added _parse_ts(s)->datetime helper that catches ValueError and raises HTTPException(422) with offending value, replaced bare datetime.fromisoformat key in min/max. All 40 tests pass. All 14 steps complete.
